purpose: Demonstrate the schema-handshake fix: once the AO evidence file is translated into the validator's input schema (gated by my adversarial QA verdicts), the rows validate cleanly. Proves the user's thesis that the raw fleet/AO files 'failed mostly on schema mismatch, not because every row is bad.'

translation_mapping.qa_status=pass_ready_for_validator: status=classified, assigned_tier=corrected_tier_if_obvious||fleet_tier, confidence floored to medium (classified cannot be low)
translation_mapping.evidence_basis: brand-locator URL -> locator; other URL present -> web_verified; no URL -> ao_cluster (artifact)

validate_only_default.error_class: DB re-review guard only (existing ownership_tier present from wave-1)
validate_only_default.note: All 4 are the same ProCare Dental Group network already consolidated as dentist_multi in wave-1. My QA verdict for them is ALSO dentist_multi, so the guard is blocking a no-op re-review, not catching a conflict.

conclusion: CONFIRMED: the AO evidence rows are structurally valid against consolidate_census.py once translated. 81/85 pass with zero special flags; the remaining 4 are blocked only by the already-classified guard (and agree with the DB). The prior mass-failure of the raw file was a schema-handshake problem, NOT a sign that the rows are individually bad. Translation must happen BEFORE the validator can assess evidence quality. IMPORTANT CAVEAT: validator PASS != consolidation-approved. The validator only checks schema + DB eligibility; it does NOT re-judge evidence sufficiency. My QA gate (ao_network_evidence_QA_20260621.json) is the evidence-sufficiency authority: 41 of these 85 are needs_more_evidence and must NOT be consolidated as classified, and ~23-25 BELKIC/AQEL rows must move branded_dso(DSO/PE) -> dentist_multi(Consolidated-not-DSO) before any DB write.
